summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring the promotion of Officer Jamie McFarland to Sergeant and the swearing-in of three new police officers (Chris Whitaker, Jason Franklin, and Garrett Vanover). The board declared intent to purchase a 2026 E-1 75-foot quint aerial apparatus for approximately $1.194 million, approved OPWC grant applications for road paving totaling nearly $1.19 million, accepted several donations, approved zoning text amendments regarding property maintenance and minimum lot sizes, and promoted John Langenheim to full-time Planning and Zoning Director.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (April 23 - May 27, 2025) - passed
Motion to accept $300 in food gift cards from CPAAA to police department - passed
Motion to accept $200 Kroger gift card from CPAAA to fire department - passed
Motion to accept $25 Kroger gift card from anonymous resident to fire department - passed
Motion to accept resignation of Tristan Smiley effective April 17, 2025 - passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter EMT Abigail Maines at $17.50/hour effective June 1, 2025 - passed
Motion to approve updated records retention schedule - passed
Motion to approve flags for heroes on Township property by Goshen Lions Club - passed
Motion to authorize CPAAA to use Township logo on t-shirts - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-009-2025 for OPWC application for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-010-2025 establishing Tax Incentive Review Council with Administrator Rose and Trustee Allen as representatives - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-011-2025 for zoning text amendments on property maintenance (grass, junk, debris, unregistered vehicles) - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-012-2025 for zoning text amendments on minimum lot sizes for R1-R5 zones - passed
Motion to declare intent to purchase 2026 E-1 75-foot quint at $1,194,188 - passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:19 PM - passed
Motion to renew David Frisby Jr. as Zoning Commission alternate with term expiring June 13, 2027 - passed
Motion to promote John Langenheim to full-time Planning and Zoning Director - passed
Motion to terminate part-time firefighter EMT Scott Harley effective May 13, 2025 - passed
Motion to terminate part-time firefighter EMT Austin Cannon effective May 13, 2025 - passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:32 PM - passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to prepare and submit OPWC application for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving project
Administrator Rose to bring resolution with actual financing numbers for the quint purchase at next meeting
Chief Myers to continue coordinating the quint purchase process and financing bids
Schedule zoning work session to discuss PUD, apartments, lot width, and related items
Schedule review of strategic plan progress
Service department to block Linton Road during next year's Memorial Day parade
Director Langenheim to advance zoning text amendments through public hearing process
Administrator Rose and Trustee Allen to serve on Tax Incentive Review Council meeting August 6
key_amounts: $300 in food gift cards donated by CPAAA to police department
$200 Kroger gift card donated by CPAAA to fire department
$25 Kroger gift card donated by anonymous resident to fire department
$17.50/hour rate for new part-time firefighter EMT Abigail Maines
$1,186,967 total cost for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving (3 miles)
$931,967 tentative OPWC grant funds for road paving
$255,000 local match for OPWC road paving project
$1,011,000 current projected cost of a new engine
$1,229,000 projected 2029 cost of a new engine
$1,194,188 cost of 2026 E-1 75-foot quint aerial apparatus
notable_topics: Promotion of Officer Jamie McFarland to Sergeant after 27 years of service
Swearing-in of three new police officers: Chris Whitaker, Jason Franklin, and Garrett Vanover
Station 18 construction progress - steel structure completion and roof starting June 2
Memorial Day parade and cemetery comments
OPWC grant application for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving
Aerial apparatus (quint) purchase presentation and intent to purchase
Zoning text amendments for property maintenance enforcement and minimum lot sizes
Promotion of John Langenheim to full-time Planning and Zoning Director
Termination of two part-time firefighter EMTs
CPAAA donations and t-shirt logo authorization
Flags for Heroes program by Goshen Lions Club
notes: Meeting had a strong ceremonial component with police promotion and swearing-in ceremonies drawing a full room including officers from neighboring departments (Miami Township, Milford). A 1998 photo of Jamie McFarland was displayed showing Trustee Tim Hodges (father of current Trustee Dan Hodges). The CPAAA provided refreshments downstairs during a recess. The board entered executive session to discuss personnel matters. Station 18 construction is progressing rapidly with steel structure near completion. Former full-time zoning inspector Alyssa Hundley departed, prompting Langenheim's promotion to full-time. The flag was noted to be on the wrong side of the room at the start of the meeting, prompting lighthearted jokes.
title: BOT 5-27-25
